summary:
The primary factor determining collagen’s strength lies in glycine’s role within the repetitive tripeptide sequence; it ensures precise alignment and compactness necessary for forming an intact triple helix that resists deformation under stress. Glycine provides the foundational molecular architecture needed for high-tensile-strength fibers despite contributions from other modified amino acids like hydroxyproline.

\boxed{B}

Glycine at every third position enables tight packing of collagen α-chains, facilitating formation of a mechanically strong triple helix. This specific arrangement is indispensable for maintaining structural integrity against tensional stresses inherent in connective tissues.\boxed{B}
